name: Australian Black Summer
impact: 18.6M hectares burned, 3 billion animals affected
desc: The 2019-2020 Australian bushfire season burned an unprecedented area, releasing 830 million tonnes of CO₂ — more than Australia's annual fossil fuel emissions.

name: Amazon Fires
impact: Record deforestation fires in the Brazilian Amazon
desc: Over 100,000 fire hotspots detected in the Amazon in 2020, driven by illegal deforestation and drought.

name: California Wildfires
impact: 4.2M acres burned, largest wildfire season in modern history
desc: The August Complex Fire alone burned over 1 million acres, becoming the first 'gigafire' in modern California history.

name: COP26 Glasgow
impact: Glasgow Climate Pact — phase down coal, double adaptation finance
desc: 197 countries adopted the Glasgow Climate Pact, the first COP decision to explicitly mention fossil fuels.

name: Antalya Wildfire
impact: 60,000+ ha burned, NDVI crash 0.72 → 0.18
desc: The Manavgat wildfire devastated ancient Mediterranean pine forests. The burn scar is still visible from space. COP31 will be held in this city in November 2026.

name: Pakistan Floods
impact: 1,739 deaths, one-third of Pakistan submerged
desc: Unprecedented monsoon rains, amplified by climate change, submerged one-third of Pakistan. Damages exceeded $30 billion.

name: Horn of Africa Drought
impact: 36.4 million people affected across 5 consecutive failed rainy seasons
desc: The worst drought in 40 years devastated East Africa, triggering mass displacement and food insecurity across Somalia, Ethiopia, and Kenya.

name: COP27 Sharm el-Sheikh
impact: Loss and Damage Fund established for the first time
desc: A historic breakthrough: wealthy nations agreed to establish a fund for climate-vulnerable countries suffering loss and damage.

name: Canada Wildfires
impact: 18.5M hectares burned — double the previous record
desc: Canada's 2023 wildfire season shattered all records. Smoke blanketed cities across North America and crossed the Atlantic to Europe.

name: Maui Wildfire
impact: 101 deaths, historic Lahaina destroyed
desc: Wind-driven wildfire destroyed the historic town of Lahaina, becoming the deadliest U.S. wildfire in over a century.

name: Libya Floods (Storm Daniel)
impact: 11,000+ deaths, city of Derna devastated
desc: Storm Daniel caused catastrophic flooding when two dams collapsed in Derna. Climate change intensified Mediterranean rainfall by up to 50%.

name: COP28 Dubai
impact: First Global Stocktake — 'transition away from fossil fuels'
desc: The first Global Stocktake called for transitioning away from fossil fuels. Loss and Damage Fund operationalized with initial pledges.

name: Amazon Drought
impact: Record low river levels, 17M hectares of forest fire-affected
desc: The Amazon experienced its worst drought on record. Rivers reached historic lows, stranding communities and triggering massive forest fires.

name: Spain (Valencia) Floods
impact: 220+ deaths, worst Spanish flood disaster in decades
desc: A year's worth of rain fell in hours in Valencia region, causing catastrophic flash flooding. Climate change made the event more likely and more intense.

name: COP29 Baku
impact: New Collective Quantified Goal — $300B/year climate finance by 2035
desc: Countries agreed to a new climate finance goal of at least $300 billion per year by 2035, though developing nations called it insufficient.

name: COP30 Belém
impact: Amazon COP — new NDCs due, nature-based solutions focus
desc: Held in the heart of the Amazon, COP30 marks the deadline for updated NDCs and puts biodiversity and indigenous rights center stage.

name: COP31 Antalya
impact: Earth Love United at COP31 — AI + human climate action
desc: COP31 comes to Antalya, Turkey — a city still recovering from the 2021 wildfires. Earth Love United is bringing AI-powered climate visualization and community restoration to the world stage.

name: Sri Lanka — Northern Province Restoration
impact: Multilayer afforestation in post-conflict degraded land
desc: Earth Love United's restoration project in Northern Province uses multilayer afforestation to rebuild ecosystems on land degraded by decades of conflict.

name: Benin — Ouidah Mangrove Restoration
impact: Mangrove restoration — 950 tC/ha, highest carbon density on Earth
desc: Restoring mangrove forests in the Ouidah Wetlands. Mangroves store more carbon per hectare than any other ecosystem on Earth.

name: Borneo — West Kalimantan Peat Swamp
impact: Peat swamp lost 96% carbon to oil palm — NDVI lies green
desc: The greenest lie on Earth: NDVI shows 0.65 (healthy green), but carbon density dropped from 1,400 to 50 tC/ha when peat swamp was replaced by oil palm.
